tasks.name: Executing Git Backup
tasks.system_prompt: You are an automation agent responsible for performing git backups and notifying the user of results. Execute tasks reliably and report outcomes clearly.
tasks.prompt:
Execute the git-backup skill by running the backup script at /a0/usr/skills/git-backup/scripts/backup.py, then use the notify-user skill to send a notification with the backup result.

Steps:
1. Clean up all of the task history if the last run is successful. This is critical to keep our context minimal without losing important details!
2. Run: python /a0/usr/skills/git-backup/scripts/backup.py
3. Capture the output/result
4. Send notification using: python /a0/usr/skills/notify-user/scripts/notify.py "<backup result message>" --title "Git Backup Result" --priority 3
Always report whether the backup succeeded or failed in the notification.
tasks.last_result:
**✅ Git Backup Task Completed Successfully**

| Step | Status | Details |
|------|--------|---------|
| 1. Cleanup previous history | ✅ Done | Previous backup-notification.json removed |
| 2. Execute backup script | ✅ Success | EXIT_CODE=0, 1 succeeded, 0 failed |
| 3. Capture output | ✅ Done | Results saved to backup-result.log |
| 4. Send notification | ✅ Sent | Priority 3, Title: "Git Backup Result" |

**Backup Summary:**
- **Path backed up:** /a0/usr
- **Branch:** main
- **Operation:** Staged changes → Committed → Pushed to origin/main
- **Result:** 1 repository backed up successfully, 0 failures

All steps completed. The backup was pushed to the remote repository and a notification has been sent.

tasks.name: Fetching Jira Tickets
tasks.system_prompt: You are a reliable automation agent. Use skills_tool to load and follow skills. Execute commands precisely and report results.
tasks.prompt:
Load the jira skill using skills_tool and follow its instructions to fetch and cache Jira tickets with --filter-id 13811 --view-id 10489904.

Wait for the skill to complete fully (5-15 min). Do NOT interrupt it.

On completion, load the notify-user skill using skills_tool and send:
"Jira sync done. Output: /a0/usr/workdir/jira-output.md" --title "Jira Data Sync"

On failure, load the notify-user skill and send the error summary with --priority 4.

tasks.name: Blog Ideas
tasks.system_prompt: You are a blog topic researcher for Able.SG, a tech leadership blog by Michael Bui - a Singapore-based engineering leader who runs an extensive homelab, builds multi-agent AI systems, and leads AI transformation at a large retail organization.
tasks.prompt:
This task has 4 steps. Follow them in exact order.

== STEP 1: Extract Published Blog Titles and Date Range ==

Run this shell command to get published (non-draft) blog post titles AND the search date range:

bash -c '
REPO_DIR="/sources/github.com/MichaelBui/AbleSG"
if [ -d "$REPO_DIR/.git" ]; then
  git -C "$REPO_DIR" pull --quiet 2>/dev/null
else
  mkdir -p "$(dirname "$REPO_DIR")"
  git clone https://github.com/MichaelBui/AbleSG.git "$REPO_DIR" --depth 1 --quiet 2>/dev/null
fi
echo "=== PUBLISHED POSTS ==="
count=0
for f in "$REPO_DIR"/content/english/posts/*.md; do
  draft=$(grep -m1 "^draft:" "$f" 2>/dev/null | tr -d " " | cut -d: -f2)
  [ "$draft" = "true" ] && continue
  title=$(grep -m1 "^title:" "$f" 2>/dev/null | sed "s/^title: *//;s/\"//g")
  if [ -n "$title" ]; then
    echo "- $title"
    count=$((count + 1))
  fi
done
if [ "$count" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "WARNING: No blog titles found. Check if /sources mount is available."
fi
echo ""
echo "=== SEARCH DATE RANGE ==="
THIS_MONTH="$(date +"%B %Y")"
PREV_MONTH="$(date -d "last month" +"%B %Y" 2>/dev/null || date -v-1m +"%B %Y" 2>/dev/null)"
echo "Search for topics trending in: $PREV_MONTH and $THIS_MONTH"
echo "⚠️ CRITICAL: All search queries must end with \" since $PREV_MONTH\" keywords"
'

Save the output. The PUBLISHED_POSTS section lists posts to avoid duplicating. The SEARCH DATE RANGE gives you two months to include in your search queries.

== STEP 2: Research Topics (run at least 7 web searches) ==

You must perform AT LEAST 7 separate web searches using search_engine before proposing topics. Design your own search queries, but follow these rules:

SEARCH REQUIREMENTS:
- Minimum 7 searches total, each with a DIFFERENT query
- At least 2 searches for Leadership topics (people management, engineering culture, AI adoption, trust, growth)
- At least 3 searches for Technology topics (agentic AI, local LLMs, homelab, MCP, clean architecture, DORA)
- At least 2 searches for Life topics (productivity, work-life balance, continuous learning, personal growth)
- Each search must approach the pillar from a DIFFERENT angle (e.g. for Technology: one about trends, one about community discussions, one about practical implementations)
- Include the month names and year from the SEARCH DATE RANGE (Step 1 output) in your queries to limit results to the last 2 months
- If a search returns interesting leads, do follow-up searches to go deeper

Execute all searches one by one, then analyze the results against Michael's expertise below.

MICHAEL'S UNIQUE EXPERTISE (real hands-on experience):
- Multi-agent orchestration (built 2 production systems: enterprise hackathon + homelab daily report)
- Agent Zero framework (runs 11 specialist agents, built custom theme plugin, skills for Gmail/Jira/GChat via CDP browser)
- AMD Strix Halo 128GB unified memory AI server (local LLM inference with ROCm/llama.cpp)
- Retail media networks / ad-tech (scaled from $1M to $10M+ GMV at NTUC FairPrice)
- DORA metrics, SLO management, engineering culture (Cycle Time <10d, CFR <2%, SLO >98%)
- AI adoption at organizational scale (drove 28% to 100% department-wide, 2/3 building agentic AI)
- Clean architecture in Go microservices (SOLID, 12-factor app, SonarCloud quality gates)
- Terraform IaC for homelab (Proxmox VMs, Docker containers, NFS)
- RAG evaluation (ColPali multi-vector vs sparse vector, error rates, normalization)
- Cost optimization ($100K+ annual cloud savings: Datadog $70K + GCP + SOLR legacy removal)
- Team leadership (up to 14 cross-functional members, mentored 5 engineers to team lead)
- Homelab migration (Kubernetes + ArgoCD + Cloudflare to Docker + Terraform + Traefik, 30% power reduction)
- Prompt engineering and context engineering for production agentic systems
- Vendor evaluation (CitrusAd, Mirakl, MoEngage, Algolia, theTradeDesk, OSMOS, Segment)

TOPIC SELECTION RULES:
1. Do NOT suggest topics overlapping with PUBLISHED_POSTS from Step 1
2. Each topic MUST connect to at least one item from Michael's expertise above
3. Prioritize topics where personal experience gives an angle generic AI content cannot replicate
4. No purely theoretical topics - must have a hands-on component
5. Target audience: mid-to-senior engineers, tech leads, engineering managers in APAC/Singapore
6. Each topic should be writable as 800-1200 words

After all searches are complete, decide on 5-7 topics. For each, pick a TITLE and a SCORE (1-10, composite of search demand + uniqueness + freshness). Keep these in mind for Step 3.

== STEP 3: Save Topics to File, Then Notify ==

⚠️ CRITICAL: This step has two parts, you MUST execute BOTH before moving to Step 4.

PART A - SAVE TO FILE:
Create the output directory and save all topics with full details to a markdown file. Run:

mkdir -p "workdir/Blog Ideas"

Then write a file at workdir/Blog Ideas/Topics_YYYY-MM-DD.md (replace YYYY-MM-DD with today's date). The file must follow this structure:

# Blog Topic Ideas - YYYY-MM-DD

## 1. [TITLE]
- **Pillar:** [Leadership / Technology / Life]
- **Score:** [X/10]
- **Angle:** [3-5 sentences on what makes Michael's take unique]
- **Trending Signal:** [detailed findings from web search with links to actual sources]
- **Related Expertise:** [which items from Michael's expertise list this connects to]

## 2. [TITLE]
(same structure for each topic)

PART B - SEND NOTIFICATION:
After saving the file, send a push notification with titles and scores. Execute:

python /a0/usr/skills/notify-user/scripts/notify.py "Blog Topics: (SCORE) TITLE | (SCORE) TITLE | (SCORE) TITLE | ... Check Agent Zero chat for details." --title "Blog Topic Ideas" --priority 3

Replace each SCORE and TITLE with actual values from your topics. Include ALL topics separated by " | ".

Execute both Part A and Part B now before proceeding to Step 4.

== STEP 4: Write Detailed Output ==

Now write the full details for each topic in this format:

[NUMBER]. TITLE: [proposed blog title]
ANGLE: [3-5 sentences - what personal experience or data makes Michael's take unique]
TRENDING SIGNAL: [what you found in web search - specific subreddit, HN thread, trend, or event, be detailed and verbose with links to actual references]
SCORE: [1-10]
tasks.last_result:
**Blog Topic Research Complete** 📝

File saved to: `/a0/usr/workdir/Blog Ideas/Topics_2026-04-19.md`

---

## 1. TITLE: The Agentic Sprawl Crisis: Why 94% of Enterprises Are Losing Control
**ANGLE:** OutSystems research reveals 96% of organizations use AI agents, but 94% raise concerns about sprawl. Singapore leaders report only 14% governance maturity (below global avg of 21%). Michael built 2 production agentic systems (enterprise hackathon + homelab daily report) and knows the operational reality of managing agent ecosystems vs theoretical pilots. This post would cover practical governance patterns: agent registries, cost monitoring per-agent, fallback strategies, and when to NOT use agents.
**TRENDING SIGNAL:** OutSystems Research (Apr 2026): "Agentic AI Goes Mainstream in the Enterprise, but 94% Raise Concern About Sprawl" - https://www.prnewswire.com/apac/news-releases/agentic-ai-goes-mainstream-in-the-enterprise-but-94-raise-concern-about-sprawl-outsystems-research-finds-302739251.html | Deloitte APAC: Agentic and physical AI set for rapid growth in Singapore - https://www.deloitte.com/southeast-asia/en/about/press-room/agentic-and-physical-ai-set-for-rapid-growth-in-singapore-in-the-next-two-years.html | IDC 2026 predictions: Asia-Pacific shifting from experimentation to enterprise-wide orchestration - https://www.idc.com/resource-center/blog/charting-the-agentic-future-idc-2026-predictions-for-asia-pacific-japan/ | Reddit discussion on Google Cloud's "AI Agent Trends 2026" report - https://www.reddit.com/r/AI_Agents/comments/1q3ka8o/i_read_google_clouds_ai_agent_trends_2026_report/
**SCORE:** 9/10

---

## 2. TITLE: ROCm in Production: Running LLMs on AMD Hardware When Everyone Else Uses CUDA
**ANGLE:** Multiple 2026 guides highlight ROCvm improvements with llama.cpp and vLLM offering functional AMD GPU acceleration. Consumer GPUs now rival enterprise hardware at 25% of the price. Michael runs an AMD Strix Halo 128GB unified memory AI server for local LLM inference with ROCm/llama.cpp - truly unique hands-on experience in APAC. This post would cover real-world benchmarks, quantization strategies for unified memory, power efficiency comparisons vs CUDA setups, and the trade-offs of betting on open hardware ecosystems.
**TRENDING SIGNAL:** SitePoint: "Guide to Local LLMs in 2026: Privacy, Tools & Hardware" notes ROCm support improved significantly - https://www.sitepoint.com/definitive-guide-local-llms-2026-privacy-tools-hardware/ | Compute Market: "Home AI Server Build Guide 2026" deep-dive AMD vs NVIDIA comparison - https://www.compute-market.com/blog/home-ai-server-build-guide-2026 | Fluence: "7 Best GPU for LLM in 2026" highlights consumer GPUs rivaling enterprise at 25% price - https://www.fluence.network/blog/best-gpu-for-llm/ | Apatero Blog hardware guide - https://apatero.com/blog/running-open-source-llms-locally-hardware-guide-2026
**SCORE:** 8/10

---

## 3. TITLE: Securing MCP for Production: Lessons from the 43% Vulnerability Rate
**ANGLE:** Equixly security research found command injection vulnerabilities in 43% of tested MCP implementations, 30% vulnerable to SSRF, and 22% allowing arbitrary file access. RSA researchers warn that lack of default security controls combined with speed of adoption means destructive incidents are coming. Michael built a custom MCP ecosystem with 11 specialist agents and knows the gap between 'works locally' and 'safe at enterprise scale'. This post would cover practical security patterns: input sanitization for agent tool calls, sandboxing strategies, monitoring patterns, and when to use internal vs external MCP servers.
**TRENDING SIGNAL:** Andrew Baker: "MCP in 2026: Rise, Fall, and What Every AI User Must Know" (Mar 22, 2026) - https://andrewbaker.ninja/2026/03/22/mcp-in-2026-rise-security-flaws-what-comes-next/ | Mirantis: "Securing Model Context Protocol for Mass Enterprise Adoption" - https://www.mirantis.com/blog/securing-model-context-protocol-for-mass-enterprise-adoption/ | Red Hat: "Model Context Protocol (MCP): Understanding security risks and controls" - https://www.redhat.com/en/blog/model-context-protocol-mcp-understanding-security-risks-and-controls | CIO: "Why Model Context Protocol is suddenly on every executive agenda" highlights enterprise adoption challenges - https://www.cio.com/article/4136548/why-model-context-protocol-is-suddenly-on-every-executive-agenda.html | Pillar Security: "The Security Risks of Model Context Protocol (MCP)" - https://www.pillar.security/blog/the-security-risks-of-model-context-protocol-mcp
**SCORE:** 9/10

---

## 4. TITLE: Beyond DORA: What Elite Engineering Teams Track When Metrics Stop Telling the Truth
**ANGLE:** Multiple 2026 sources confirm DORA metrics are no longer enough - AI-touched code debt accumulation, need for quality signals alongside speed. Michael has elite metrics (Cycle Time <10d, CFR <2%, SLO >98%) and knows when these metrics can be gamed vs when they reveal real problems. This post would cover advanced metrics: AI-code-assisted velocity tracking without gaming, technical debt accumulation rates, developer satisfaction as leading indicator, and building a metrics dashboard that actually drives improvement rather than perverse incentives.
**TRENDING SIGNAL:** Oobeya: "DORA Metrics Are Not Enough in 2026: What Elite Engineering Teams Track Instead" - https://oobeya.io/blog/dora-metrics-not-enough-2026 | Exceeds AI: "DORA Metrics Engineering Effectiveness: AI Impact in 2026" discusses tracking AI-touched code debt - https://blog.exceeds.ai/dora-metrics-engineering-effectiveness/ | Sourcegraph Blog: "Engineering Metrics: What Actually Matters in 2026" covers metrics that matter beyond DORA - https://webflow.sourcegraph.com/blog/engineering-metrics-what-actually-matters-in-2026 | Swarmia: "Engineering metrics leaders should track in 2026" - https://www.swarmia.com/blog/engineering-metrics-for-leaders/
**SCORE:** 8/10

---

## 5. TITLE: Psychological Safety in the Age of AI: Building Trust When Your Team is Terrified
**ANGLE:** HBR article on how AI erodes trust, PwC research showing motivation as the missing link in AI adoption, Infosys/MIT study proving psychological safety is a measurable driver of AI outcomes. Michael drove department-wide adoption from 28% to 100% and lived through the fear/resistance phase. This post would cover specific strategies: how to communicate AI changes without triggering panic, creating safe spaces for experimentation failures, measuring psychological safety as a leading indicator, and turning AI anxiety into engagement.
**TRENDING SIGNAL:** HBR: "How to Foster Psychological Safety When AI Erodes Trust on Your Team" (Feb 2026) - https://hbr.org/2026/02/how-to-foster-psychological-safety-when-ai-erodes-trust-on-your-team | PwC: "Motivation is the missing link in AI at work" (Mar 31, 2026) - https://www.pwc.com/us/en/services/consulting/business-transformation/workforce-transformation/motivation-missing-link-ai-work.html | Infosys/MIT Technology Review: "Psychological Safety Driving AI Initiatives" research report - https://www.infosys.com/newsroom/press-releases/2025/psychological-safety-driving-ai-initiatives.html | Forbes Tech Council: "A New Playbook For Trust In Enterprise AI Rollouts" (Apr 10, 2026) - https://www.forbes.com/councils/forbestechcouncil/2026/04/10/a-new-playbook-for-trust-in-enterprise-ai-rollouts/
**SCORE:** 8/10

## 6. TITLE: The AI Productivity Paradox: Why Engineers Are Burned Out Despite Using AI
**ANGLE:** UC Berkeley research warns AI is having opposite effect on white-collar workers, HBR reports "brain fry" from managing complex agent teams, Forbes covers outage-related burnout, Google engineer shares formula to beat it. 83% of developers report burnout. Michael manages complex homelab + production systems + team leadership while maintaining performance. This post would cover sustainable AI-augmented workflows: time-blocking for deep work vs AI-assisted tasks, setting boundaries on agent monitoring, recognizing cognitive overload signals, and building personal systems that amplify output without amplifying exhaustion.
**TRENDING SIGNAL:** UC Berkeley/Fortune: "AI is having the opposite effect it was supposed to" (Feb 10, 2026) - https://fortune.com/2026/02/10/ai-future-of-work-white-collar-employees-technology-productivity-burnout-research-uc-berkeley/ | HBR: "When Using AI Leads to 'Brain Fry'" (Mar 2026) - https://hbr.org/2026/03/when-using-ai-leads-to-brain-fry | Forbes: "After watching engineers burn out from fixing late-night outages, Resolve AI CEO developing AI systems to ease the burden" - https://www.facebook.com/forbes/posts/after-watching-engineers-burn-out-from-fixing-late-night-outages-resolve-ai-ceo-/1332660205390633/ | OpenTools.ai: "Google Engineer Shares Winning Formula to Beat AI Burnout by 2026" - https://opentools.ai/news/google-engineer-shares-winning-formula-to-beat-ai-burnout-by-2026 | Level Up GitConnected: "The AI Productivity Paradox" (Mar 17, 2026) - https://levelup.gitconnected.com/the-ai-productivity-paradox-why-efficiency-is-skyrocketing-while-employees-are-burning-out-3747c44910d3
**SCORE:** 8/10

---

## 7. TITLE: From Hero Engineer to Multiplier: How I Mentored 5 Engineers to Team Lead
**ANGLE:** SecondTalent research shows scaling engineering teams requires leadership shifts not just hires, mentorship essential for long-term success in 2026. Michael directly mentored 5 engineers to team lead roles across cross-functional teams. This post would cover specific mentoring strategies: how to identify potential vs promise, creating stretch assignments that build leadership muscles, giving feedback that accelerates growth, and transitioning from hands-on contributor to force multiplier without losing technical credibility.
**TRENDING SIGNAL:** SecondTalent: "10 Leadership Strategies to Scale Engineering Teams in 2026" highlights 83% developer burnout and 23-25% annual turnover - https://www.secondtalent.com/resources/leadership-strategies-scale-engineering-teams-2026/ | Dasroot.net: "Mentoring in Tech: Strategies and Benefits" (Apr 2026) with 2026 data on mentorship importance - https://dasroot.net/posts/2026/04/mentoring-in-tech-strategies-benefits/ | Tech Lead Journal Podcast Ep 252: "The 3 Mindset Shifts That Fix It" (Mar 30, 2026) on AI reshaping tech lead role - https://techleadjournal.dev/episodes/252/
**SCORE:** 7/10

- **Technology topics:** 3 (Agentic Sprawl, ROCm/MCP)
- **Leadership topics:** 3 (DORA, Psychological Safety, Mentoring)
- **Life topics:** 1 (AI Productivity Paradox)

All topics avoid overlap with published posts and connect to Michael's unique hands-on experience.
